author: Niche Gamer
text:
Dragon’s Dogma 2 finally adds unlimited fast travel Capcom has released a major update for Dragon’s Dogma 2 titled Dark Arisen, and it tackles one of the biggest complaints players have had since launch: fast travel. The addition is the Eternal Ferrystone, a new item that grants unlimited fast travel. You will receive it early in the adventure after meeting Brant during the […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Stonkrider turns real stock market charts into chaotic dirt bike tracks A browser game called Stonkrider is quickly becoming one of the internet’s latest viral obsessions, and its premise is almost too simple not to work. Created by a Reddit user over a single weekend, Stonkrider transforms real stock data into motocross-style tracks. Every rise and fall in a stock’s price history becomes part of the […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
UK looks to ban children from using voice chat in games like Roblox and Fortnite Under new Online Safety Act proposals, the UK government may ban children from using voice chat with strangers in games such as Fortnite and Roblox. The idea comes as British officials continue looking for ways to improve child safety online. Online Safety Minister Kanishka Narayan reportedly said the government is considering additional restrictions aimed at […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Overwatch reveals new character Shion, a motorcycle-riding DPS hero for season 3 Blizzard has officially revealed Shion, the newest hero joining Overwatch when Season 3: Into the Tiger’s Den launches on June 16. As Overwatch’s 52nd playable hero, Shion enters the roster as a Damage-class flanker tied directly to the game’s ongoing Talon and Hashimoto Clan storyline. She’s portrayed as one of the most feared leaders within […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
XBOX explains what “console exclusive” really means in 2026 When Xbox CEO Asha Sharma called Gears of War: E-Day an “Xbox console exclusive” during the recent Xbox Games Showcase, plenty of gamers were left wondering what that actually meant. Now, Xbox Chief Content Officer Matt Booty has cleared things up. According to a new interview (via GameInformer), “console exclusive” simply means the game won’t […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Persona 4 Revival’s new voice cast leaves previous Chie actor “grieving” The latest trailer for Persona 4 Revival finally gave fans a closer look at Atlus’ upcoming remake, but it wasn’t just the updated visuals that caught attention. While Atlus still hasn’t officially revealed the actors behind Persona 4 Revival’s main characters, it’s now clear that the cast has been completely recast. That’s not entirely surprising. […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Fumito Ueda says Bluepoint Games closure is sad and disappointing Few studios earned as much respect for remakes as Bluepoint Games, which is why its closure earlier this year came as a shock to many across the industry. Now, Shadow of the Colossus creator Fumito Ueda has shared his thoughts on the situation, describing the news as both disappointing and sad. Speaking in a recent […]

text:
Aerial arcade combat game Wind Runners launches in September Publisher Twin Sails Interactive and developer Ludic Studios announced a release date for Wind Runners, their aerial arcade combat game. Wind Runners is set to launch in early access later this year for Windows PC (Steam) on September 23rd. A playable demo is available now. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus a new trailer: […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Ghibli-inspired adventure game Into the Wind announced Publisher Three Friends and developer Bloom & Gloom have announced Into the Wind, a nostalgic Studio Ghibli-inspired adventure game where you run deliveries with a sentient motorcycle-plane. In the game you’ll zip around an idyllic island reminiscent of Ghibli’s films, where you’ll do deliveries, make friends, battle air pirates, decorate your home, explore the islands, and try […]

text:
9 R.I.P. sequel western release launches in fall 2026 Publisher Idea Factory International has announced a western release for 9 R.I.P. sequel, the sequel to the original 9 R.I.P. game. 9 R.I.P. sequel is coming west sometime this fall for Nintendo Switch, digitally and both standard and limited editions. Here’s a rundown on the game: Following the endings of 9 R.I.P., Misa Isshiki finds herself at a crossroads with navigating […]

text:
Folklore platformer game Kalanoro launches in August Publisher New Tales and developer Red Raketa Studio announced a release date for Kalanoro, their colorful Madagascar folklore platformer game. Kalanoro is set to launch for Windows PC (via Steam), Xbox Series X|S, Switch, and PS5 on August 27th, worldwide. A demo is available on its Steam page. text:
Mousebusters launches for Switch and Xbox this month Japanese developer Odencat has announced Mousebusters is coming to Switch and Xbox later this month. The new Complete Edition for Mousebusters was just released for Windows PC (Steam) and is coming to Switch and Xbox Series X|S on June 25th. Here’s a rundown on the previously announced game, plus a new trailer: Explore a haunted apartment building […]

text:
Hello Kitty Party Land release date set for October Bandai Namco and Sanrio Games have announced a release date for Hello Kitty Party Land. Hello Kitty Party Land is coming west for both Switch and Switch 2 this fall, on October 29th via the eShop and at retail. The Japanese version launches the same day. Here’s a rundown on the previously announced game, plus a […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
The Hidden Power of Indie Games in Shaping Gaming’s Future   Some of the most quietly impactful moments in modern gaming haven’t come from megabudget studios, but from tiny teams working against the odds. The growth and influence of indie games have redrawn the boundaries of what players can expect, introducing new art styles, gameplay mechanics, and even creative business models that big publishers are […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Why Platform Wars Shape the Way Players Experience Fortnite’s Universe   When Fortnite first exploded onto the scene, it felt like each match invited everyone in, console, PC, and mobile players converging in the same lively playground. text:
OneState update 1.1 adds new story content with actors Daniel and Louis Moncada Independent developer ChillBase has released the first major update for OneState after its full release this spring. Their popular online social roleplaying added new content with brothers / actors Daniel and Louis Moncada, famous for their role as “The Cousins” from Breaking Bad. The new 1.1 update “Under Pressure” adds a new “Dos Caminos” storyline that […]

text:
Akupara Games partners with Pixeljam and Behold Studios Akupara Games has announced two new publishing partnerships, adding indie veterans Pixeljam and Behold Studios to its growing portfolio of developers. While both studios may not be household names outside indie circles, they’ve spent years creating memorable experiences long before independent games became a major force in the industry.
